purpose: Intelligence-First migration audit — current candidate pipeline state before any migration code is written

universe_counts_by_stage.note: Day 1 correction — the earlier '~200 symbols per cycle' was ambiguous. These are the distinct counts at each pipeline stage.

stage_1_source_pool_pre_scoring_cap.description: All symbols from get_dynamic_universe() + bot-level additions before any scoring cap is applied
stage_1_source_pool_pre_scoring_cap.components: Tier A (~57) + Tier B (~50 promoted) + Tier C (variable sector rotation ~6–60) + Tier D (~150 position research) + options universe (~20–40) + favourites (13) + held (variable)
stage_1_source_pool_pre_scoring_cap.estimated_total: 250–400 symbols (many overlap between tiers — actual unique count lower)
stage_1_source_pool_pre_scoring_cap.overlap_note: Tier D names frequently appear in Tier A/B/C already. Unique count closer to 180–250 on typical days.

stage_2_pre_scoring_cap_applied.description: After signal_pipeline.py applies the global scoring cap before score_universe() runs
stage_2_pre_scoring_cap_applied.typical_count: Up to 140 symbols enter score_universe() — Tier D capped at 60 within that 140

stage_3_live_scored_all.description: all_scored — every symbol scored on any dimension (signal_pipeline.py:776)
stage_3_live_scored_all.typical_count: 60–140 symbols (all symbols that received at least one dimension score)

stage_4_above_threshold.description: scored — symbols above min_score_to_trade threshold (14 paper / 28 live)

stage_5_post_guardrails.description: After guardrails.filter_candidates() drops held, cooldown, failed-thesis, open-order, earnings-today

stage_6_apex_pre_cap_deduped.description: After dedup by symbol (best apex_cap_score wins per symbol)

stage_7_apex_final_capped.description: Final list sent to apex_call() — top 30 unconditional + slots 31-50 gated by expanded_band_floor=20

CORE_SYMBOLS.description: Macro ETFs, vol, inverse ETFs, crypto proxies, commodities

CORE_EQUITIES.description: Mega-cap equities across 10 sectors, always scored every cycle

tier_b.label: Tier B — daily promoted
tier_b.failure_mode: falls back to Tier A only if stale or missing

tier_c_sector.label: Tier C — sector rotation leaders
tier_c_sector.method: 5-day relative strength of 11 sector ETFs vs SPY
tier_c_sector.adds: top-3 sector ETFs + constituent stocks from _SECTOR_STOCKS dict (6 per ETF)

tier_c_options.label: Tier C — options universe
tier_c_options.description: Liquid optionable names pinned so options signals are always evaluated

tier_d.label: Tier D — position research universe
tier_d.description: Fundamental-quality discovery names, bypasses gap/premarket-volume promoter

score_bonus.type: sort-key adjustment only — no quota protection

held_positions.label: Held positions — always pinned
held_positions.description: Open IBKR positions pinned into universe before scoring so PM always sees them

candidate_merge_flow.output: set of symbols, all tiers merged, no labels
scored candidates with 10-dimension scores; Alpaca bars fetched per-symbol
Tier D candidates get effective_score with research bonus
Tier D candidates get sort-key bonus up to +8
one row per symbol, best apex_cap_score wins
order placed or rejected with reason

apex_output.new_entries: list of {symbol, trade_type, conviction, direction, rationale}
apex_output.higher_score_skips: audit trail for skipped high-score candidates

apex_cap_flow.sort_key: apex_cap_score (= signal score for non-Tier-D; signal score + bonus for Tier D with score >= 18)
apex_cap_flow.quota_system: none — flat sort with sort-key bonus only
apex_cap_flow.structural_protection: none — Tier D competes against all other candidates in the same pool

execution_boundary.live_data_location: signals.py score_universe() via ThreadPoolExecutor — Alpaca bars per symbol
execution_boundary.execution_data_location: orders_core.py — IBKR bid/ask + Alpaca quote at execution only
execution_boundary.comment: Current system violates the locked architecture boundary — live bot builds universe and scores broad intraday data. This will be corrected by the Intelligence-First migration.

position_candidate_disadvantage.root_cause: No reason_to_care label exists on any candidate when it reaches the Apex cap sort
position_candidate_disadvantage.mechanism: Structural Tier D names and 5-minute tactical movers compete in the same flat sorted pool
position_candidate_disadvantage.current_mitigation: apex_cap_score bonus (max +8) — sort-key nudge only, no quota protection
position_candidate_disadvantage.failure_mode: On high-gap days, 30+ non-Tier-D names score above Tier D names even with the bonus
position_candidate_disadvantage.what_migration_fixes: reason_to_care label + structural quota (min 8 protected slots) + attention cap (max 15)

safe_shadow_wiring_point.description: After _cut_candidates is finalised, before apex_call() is invoked
safe_shadow_wiring_point.side_effects: none — read-only comparison, writes to data/universe_builder/ only

feature_flags_added_this_session.purpose: master switch for all shadow output
production wiring — never enabled during this work
